facts.statement: T-Mobile US, Inc. is classified in SEC metadata under SIC description "Radiotelephone Communications" and trades on Nasdaq under the ticker TMUS.
The company reported FY2025 revenue of $78.558 billion.
The company reported FY2025 net income of $8.317 billion.
The company reported FY2025 net cash provided by operating activities of $18.559 billion.
The company reported FY2025 long-term debt of $74.197 billion.
The company reported FY2025 cash and cash equivalents of $5.409 billion.
The company reported FY2025 stockholders' equity of $69.656 billion.
The 10-K filing identifies revenue categories including branded postpaid revenue, branded prepaid revenue, wholesale and other service revenue, service revenue, and product equipment revenue.
The 10-K also tracks product and service revenue categories such as product and service other, indicating the company reports both service and equipment-related revenue streams.

In the proxy appendix, management reported FY2025 Adjusted Free Cash Flow of $17.995 billion and said it is used to evaluate cash available to pay debt, repurchase shares, pay dividends, and provide further investment in the business.

Management said Adjusted EBITDA and Core Adjusted EBITDA are used internally to monitor financial performance, allocate resources, evaluate and compensate personnel, and benchmark performance versus competitors.
The proxy appendix reported FY2025 Adjusted EBITDA of $33.937 billion and Core Adjusted EBITDA of $33.924 billion.

The proxy appendix says T-Mobile began implementing network restructuring initiatives in Q4 2025 to rationalize network and backhaul services and eliminate duplicative costs, with related costs including network decommissioning and contract termination costs.
The proxy appendix says legal-related expenses and recoveries include settlement of certain litigation and compliance costs associated with the August 2021 cyberattack, net of insurance recoveries.
The proxy appendix says FY2025 other, net included $390 million of severance and related costs associated with the 2025 workforce transformation.
As of March 31, 2026, Deutsche Telekom held voting control over approximately 54.5% of T-Mobile's outstanding common stock and was described as the company's controlling stockholder.
Under the Second Amended and Restated Stockholders' Agreement dated June 22, 2020, and based on its ownership position, Deutsche Telekom has the right to designate 10 members of the board.

The proxy states the board includes independent directors, including three designated by the Nominating, Corporate Governance and Compliance Committee, and that each meets applicable NASDAQ and SEC independence standards.
The 10-K signature page identifies Srinivasan Gopalan as Chief Executive Officer and Peter Osvaldik as Chief Financial Officer; the proxy also lists G. Michael Sievert among the director nominees.

The 10-K index identifies the 2023 Incentive Award Plan, forms of restricted stock unit award agreements, a non-qualified deferred executive compensation plan, an executive continuity plan, and an amended executive incentive compensation recoupment policy as governance and compensation arrangements.

For the 2026 annual meeting, stockholders are asked to elect 13 director nominees, ratify Deloitte & Touche LLP as independent auditor for fiscal 2026, and hold an advisory vote on named executive officer compensation for 2025.

The proxy's forward-looking statement section highlights risks including competition, cyberattacks, spectrum scarcity and renewal risk, debt servicing risk, regulatory change, inability to obtain regulatory approvals for transactions, supply-chain disruptions, and foreign ownership limitations affecting equity financing outside the United States.
